D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

ISO Invariance — Push in RAW vs Crank the ISO

on an ISO-invariant sensor, shooting underexposed at ISO 100 and pushing the RAW +5 stops is as good as shooting at ISO 3200; on a non-invariant sensor, the push wins or loses depending on where read noise enters

ISO is digital gain. An invariant sensor (Sony A7R-class) applies read-noise before the ISO multiplier — so pushing a RAW from ISO 100 by +5 stops is equivalent to shooting ISO 3200 in-camera. A non-invariant sensor (Canon 5D Mark III) applies meaningful read-noise after the gain stage — pushing RAW shows more noise than cranking ISO.

Two parallel paths: left = shot at ISO 100 with −5 EV exposure, then RAW push +5 stops. Right = shot at ISO 3200, correct exposure. Toggle invariant vs non-invariant sensor — the noise readouts diverge on the non-invariant model.
